questions.question: Explain the difference between session hijacking and session fixation attacks. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Session hijacking involves stealing an existing valid session ID
Session fixation involves forcing a user to use an attacker-known session ID before authentication
Both allow attacker access but through different mechanisms

questions.question: Describe five best practices for secure session management in web applications. (10 marks)
expectedPoints: Use cryptographically secure random session IDs of sufficient length
Implement proper cookie attributes (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite)
Regenerate session ID after authentication to prevent fixation
Set appropriate absolute and inactivity timeouts
Store session data securely on server-side
Transmit session IDs only over HTTPS
Validate sessions on server for each request
Implement session rotation for sensitive operations
Avoid exposing session IDs in URLs or logs
